title: 한국형 부영양화지수에 의한 영산강 유역의 부영양화 특성
abstract: ISixteen large-sized weirs were constructed to secure water resources through the Four Main River Project in 2012 as a drought-related strategy and two of the weirs, Seungchon-bo and Juksan-bo, were built in the Yeoungsan River basin. Due to the construction of the weirs, streamflows significantly decreased and stagnant area of water increased. Algal blooms are continuously occurring from summer to early fall every year. Based on the recent concerns of people about safe agricultural products, the agricultural water quality pollution becomes a nationwide issue. Its importance and significance are getting more attention and improvement of algal blooms in the Yeoungsan River with water quality improvement is crucial. Therefore, the present study used the Korean trophic state index evaluation method developed by National Institute of Environmental Research (NIER), Ministry of Environment in order to understand the change of eutrophication caused by the construction of Seungchon-bo and Juksan-bo, and the operation of total phosphorus treatment facility in Gwangju Metropolitan City. For the evaluation, the data collected from 12 main gage stations in streams and 8 measurement points in four lakes in the Yeoungsan River basin were divided into spring (March to May), summer (June to August), autumn (September to November), and winter (December to February). The divided data were again split into before and after 2013 to assess impacts of the Four Main River Project. The present study calculated seasonal TSI_Ko using water quality parameters of COD, T-P, and Chl-a and revealed characteristics of eutrophication by classifying trophic states into four levels.
authors: 박성천, Kimjongo, Leewoobum, Sooho Park, 이상득
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: 대구경 P.R.D 공법 및 대형 바렛파일을 이용한 현장타설말뚝 시공
abstract: 대형화 구조물이 늘어나는 추세에 따라 하중이 증가하여 이에 대한 기초에 큰 지지력을 요구하고 있다. 현장타설말뚝은 상부 하중의 크기에 따라 근입깊이를 조절 할 수 있으나 장비의 운용성 및 적용 직경의 한계가 있어 현장여건을 고려하여 적용할 필요가 있다. 본 논문에서는 상대적으로 하중이 크게 작용하는 OO플랜트 현장에서 국내최초로 P.R.D공법으로 대구경 O1,500mm 및 Barrette 공법으로는 □2,800mm×2,000mm을 적용하여 양방향 재하시험을 한 결과 시공성을 확보함과 동시에 지지력에 대한 안정성을 확보하였다.
authors: 배성완, 임대성, 전재구, 김태현

title: 건설구조물의 건전도 모니터링을 위한 무선계측 시스템의 실험적 연구
abstract: 본 연구에서는 기존 유선기반 계측시스템의 단점을 극복하고, 효율적인 구조물의 건전도 모니터링을 수행하고자 무선기반 계측 시스템을 개발하여 실험적으로 검증하였다. 개발된 시스템의 H/W는 고성능 CPU와 다종계측 제어를 위한 Multi I/O로 구성하였다. 또한 개발된 시스템의 S/W는 건전도 모니터링에 사용될 수 있도록 Labview 기반의 계측프로그램을 개발하였고, 이를 EST를 활용하여 무선계측 시스템에 임베디딩하였다. 마지막으로 모형구조물을 대상으로 유 · 무선 동적응답 계측실험을 수행하여, 개발된 무선 계측시스템의 타당성을 확인하였다.
authors: 허광희, 전준용, 전승곤, 방건혁, 이진옥

title: 건설산업 미청구공사의 위험성을 판별하는 핵심인자 추출에 대한 연구
abstract: 최근 건설사의 잇따른 영업이익 하락과 미청구공사 금액의 증가가 한국 건설산업의 재무적 이슈로 지적되고 있다. 과도하게 쌓인 미청구공사는 손실로 전이될 가능성이 높다고 지적되고 있으며, 미청구공사가 증가할 수 있는 다양한 상황이 존재하기 때문에 기업 외부 관계자가 위험성을 사전에 판단하기란 쉽지 않은 실정이다. 게다가 미청구공사의 위험도를 판단하는 선행연구 및 판단기준의 부재와 제한적인 공시정보는 미청구공사의 정확한 파악에 대한 어려움을 가중시켰고, 이는 시장혼란 및 효율성 저하와 연결된다. 따라서 본 연구의 목표는 기업 미청구공사의 보유수준이 건전한지 혹은 위험한지 파악하는데 활용할 수 있도록 재무제표 상의 계정들 중 미청구공사와 관련이 있는 핵심인자를 추출하고자 한다. 첫째로 문헌고찰과 전문가 인터뷰를 통해 변수를 선정했고, 선정된 변수에 대한 데이터를 수집하였다. 둘째로, 수집된 데이터를 매출액 분위별로 나눠서, 어떤 변수가 미청구공사에 영향을 미치는지 회귀분석을 통해 분석하였다. 또한 경영악화를 기록한 데이터에 대해서도 매출액 분위별 분석을 실시하여, 손실로 전이될 가능성이 높은 미청구공사에 영향을 미치는 변수를 확인하였다. 본 연구에서의 결과는 향후 미청구공사의 위험성을 판단하는 기준 설정에 사용될 수 있을 것이다.
authors: 유시라, 목선수, 지석호, 박주용
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: 도시부 횡단보도 제원 산정에 관한 연구 :폭과 정지선을 중심으로
abstract: 최근 우리나라는 고도의 경제 성장과 더불어 급격한 도시화로 인하여 인구, 환경, 주택문제가 발생하고 있고, 특히 교통문제는 심각한 사회 문제로 대두되고 있는 실정이다. 현재까지의 교통정책 방향은 차량소통 중심으로 시행되어 온 것이 현실이다. 이에 본 연구에서는 운전자 관점이 아닌 보행자 관점의 교통정책의 필요성을 부각시키고자 한다. 2015년 전체 교통사고 사망자수(4,621명) 대비 보행 중 사망자수(1,795명) 비율은 38.8%로 보행자 교통사고 해결 방안 수립이 요구되고 있다. 횡단보도는 보행자가 안전하게 횡단할 수 있어야 하지만 현행 횡단보도의 폭원 산정 및 정지선 설치위치에 대한 명확한 기준 없이 도로의 폭을 기준으로 설치되어 있다. 특히 보행량이 많은 대학교나 상업시설 주변 지역은 횡단보도 설계 시 보행자 통행을 고려하지 않고 설계되고 있는 실정이다. 횡단보도 녹색시간 동안 수용할 수 있는 보행자 통행량보다 실제 통행량이 많을 경우, 대기 보행자가 주어진 녹색시간 동안 횡단하지 못하거나 주어진 횡단보도 면적을 벗어난 상태로 무리하게 횡단하여 교통 안전상의 문제가 발생할 수 있다. 이에 본 연구에서는 차량소통 위주의 신호시간 결정, 보행 신호시간 부족, 대로 상에서의 보행자 횡단거리 등 많은 문제점을 내재하고 있는 횡단보도 내 보행자 사고율을 낮추고자 보행자 통행량과 보행속도를 고려한 횡단보도 제원을 산정하고자 한다. 먼저 횡단보도 제원 산정을 위해 횡단보도, 보행자, 정지선에 관한 법규와 기존 연구문헌을 고찰하였다. 기존 횡단보도 제원과 정지선의 문제점을 분석하여 횡단보도 제원 산정을 위한 방법론을 제시하고, 이를 토대로 횡단보도 제원을 산정하였다. 결론적으로 본 연구에서 제시한 횡단보도 제원 및 정지선 개선방안은 해당 교통안전시설물 설치 및 기준 정립 시 기초 자료로 활용 가능할 것으로 기대된다.
authors: 김윤미, Je Jin Park, 권성대, Ha Tae Jun
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: 연관규칙분석을 활용한 교량 부재별 손상 패턴 도출 모델 개발에 관한 기초 연구
abstract: 교량의 생애주기적 유지관리 비용을 절감시키기 위하여 교량 손상에 대한 예방적 유지관리가 필요하며, 이를 위해 교량의 성능 저하 가능성과 크기를 정량화하는 것이 필요하다. 다수의 선행연구에서는 회귀모형에 기반하여 몇 가지 대표 변수를 이용한 성능열화모델을 수립하였으나 이는 일부 대표 부재에 대한 것이며 적은 변수로 인해 부재의 개별 특성을 반영하기 어려웠다. 따라서 본 연구에서는 개별 부재들의 특성을 반영할 수 있는 다양한 변수를 고려하고 교량 손상을 부재별 손상 유형 수준에서 추정하는 패턴들을 도출하는 모델을 수립하기 위해, 빅데이터 분석 기법 중 하나인 연관규칙분석의 이용 가능성을 확인하고자 한다. 예비적 결과를 통해 확인한 일부 패턴들은 선행연구와 현실 상황에 부합하여 연관규칙분석 방법론의 적용 가능성을 확인하였으며, 향후 연구에는 사용한 Apriori의 속도와 성능 향상을 위해 군집분석 등의 방법을 추가적으로 고려할 것이다.
authors: 임소람, 정세환, 지석호, 송준호
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: 로컬영역에서의 토공장비 관제 모니터링을 위한 VR 시뮬레이터 개발
abstract: 본 연구는 국토교통과학기술진흥원 건설기술연구사업의 일환인 “ICT를 활용한 건설장비 관제 및 스마트 시공 기술 개발”(과제번호: 16SCIP-B079689-03)에 대한 지원 사업으로 수행됨.

title: 베인 전단시험기를 이용한 낙동강모래의 마찰각에 관한 연구
abstract: A vane shear test (VST) is a simple testing method for determining an undrained shear strength of cohesive soils by minimizing soil disturbance. In this study, the VST was used to determine a shear strength of sand. Dry Nakdong River sand was prepared for loose and dense conditions in a cell and then pressurized with 25, 50, 75 or 100 kPa from the surface of sand. A vane (5 cm in diameter and 10 cm in height) was rotated and a torque was measured within sand. When a torque moment by vane and friction resistance moment by sand is assumed to be equalized, a friction angle can be obtained. When a vane rotates within clay, a uniform undrained shear strength is assumed to be acting on cylindrical failure surface. On the other hand, when it is applied for sand, the failure shape can be assumed to be an octagonal or square column. The relationship between measured torque and resistant force along assumed failure shapes due to friction of sand was derived and the internal friction angle of sand was determined for loose and dense conditions. For the same soil condition, a series of direct shear test was carried out and compared with VST result. The friction angle from VST was between 24-42 degrees for loose sand and 33-53 degrees for dense sand. This is similar to those of direct shear tests.
authors: 쪼우안, 호우야우롱, 박성식
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: 한국형 인공월면토 (KLS-1) 개발을 위한 기초 연구
abstract: 최근 우주개발의 중요성이 다시 부각되면서 주요 우주개발국들은 달 토양과 물리적·화학적 특성이 유사한 인공월면토(lunar simulant)를 개발하여 각종 연구에 활용하고 있다. 달 토양은 지구 토양과 생성 매커니즘과 지반공학적거동이 다르기 때문에 완벽하게 모사하는 것이 어렵고 제작비용이 많이 소모된다. 기존 인공월면토 중 국제적으로 가장 널리 사용되고 있는 재료는 미국항공우주국(NASA)에서 제작한 JSC-1A이며, 우리나라는 아직 국제적으로 공인된 인공월면토가 없는 실정이다. 따라서 본 연구에서는 국내 지반재료를 이용한 한국형 인공월면토 개발을 위한 기초연구를 수행하여 그 시제품으로 KLS-1(Korea lunar simulant - type 1)을 제시하였다. KLS-1은 기존 국외 인공월면토들과 비교하여 유사성과 경제성이 우수하여 향후 국내 우주개발 사업뿐만 아니라 각종 국제연구에 폭 넓게 활용될수 있을 것으로 기대된다.
authors: 유병현, 한진태, 장일한
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: 충격반향법(Impact echo)를 사용한 콘크리트 배면 공동 탐지 기법에 대한 연구
abstract: 본 연구는 미래창조과학부 및 국가과학기술연구회의 융합연구사업의 일환으로 수행하였음. [융합연구단-14-2-ETRI, 사물인터넷(IoT) 기반 도시 지하매설물 모니터링 및 관리시스템 기술 개발]
authors: 송석민, 박두희, 안재광, 강재모
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: Freundlich 등온선을 사용한 염분확산 모델 적용에 따른 철근깊이 염소이온 농도 예측
abstract:
본 연구는 국토교통기술촉진사업 “해양환경 콘크리트 구조물 수명예측 소프트웨어 개발 (15CTAP-C097546-01)”
의 지원으로 수행되었으며, 이에 감사드립니다.
authors: 홍성인, 김민재, 양희준, 김한솔, 안기용
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: 연안재해 저감을 위한 친수형 호안 블록에 관한 연구
abstract: 연안재해 저감을 위한 정부 사업 중 연안침식 및 퇴적을 저감시키기 위한 연안정비 사업 1단계(2000년~2009 년)와 2단계(2010년~2019년)가 현재까지 진행중이다. 이와 같은 연안정비 사업을 수행함에 있어 연안 보호를 위한 인공 콘크리트 피복재의 설치가 필요하다. 현재 국내에서는 일본에서 개발된 제품을 현장에 적용하고 있거나, 호안보 호공 설치를 시행하는 건설사에서 자체 개발한 블록을 설치하고 있는 추세이다. 그렇지만 이에 대한 수리특성 검증 및 안정성확보 여부, 설계기법과 같은 기술적인 검토는 부족한 상황이다. 본 연구에서는 이런 점들을 보완하여 사람 들의 접근성이 좋으면서 연안재해 저감 및 보호 능력이 우수한 친수형 호안 블럭을 개발하고자 한다. 본 연구를 통 하여 호안블록의 수리특성 및 안정계수를 도출할 수 있었다.
authors: 김종길, 이광준, 이경범, 김영택
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: 수치해석을 통한 발파진동의 전파특성 분석
abstract: 이 연구는 한국도로공사에서 발주된 “KTX 경부선 하부 터널발파 공사에 따른 도로 손상영향 검토 및 DB 구축 용역”의 과제 지원으로 수행되었습니다. 이에 감사 드립니다.
authors: 안재광, 박두희, Jae-Kwang Ahn, Duhee Park

title: 도심지 대심도 동시굴착에 따른 가시설의 구조안정성에 대한 연구
abstract: 제 목 : 도심지 대심도 동시굴착에 따른 가시설의 구조 안정성 에 대한 연구 지하 굴착공사의 대상인 자연지반은 그 성상이 매우 복잡하고 다양한 특성을 가지므로 지반조건과 현장특성을 고려하지 않은 획일적인 공법선정은 시공상에 어려움 및 흙막이벽체의 붕괴를 가져올 뿐만 아니라 현장 전반의 안전에 대한 문제를 불...
authors: Doo Hwan Kim, 김상곤, 김성필, 송관권, 안만복
journal: 대한토목학회 학술대회

title: Sustainable remediation principles, guidelines and practices
abstract: Due to depletion of natural resources, increased natural disasters, waste, greenhouse gas and pollutant generation, environmental deterioration, and loss of biodiversity, geoenvironmental engineers are facing new challenges. While it is generally believed that the concepts of sustainable development including the three dimensions of environment, economy and society must be followed for protecting future generations, it is much easier said than implemented. Green remediation focusses on mainly reducing environmental impacts while sustainable remediation additionally considers social and economic aspects. Therefore, the focus of this paper is on the application of the principles and best management practices of sustainable geoenvironmental engineering to site remediation projects through frameworks, guidelines, case studies and examples of sustainable remediation practices. In addition, challenges and future needs are included. While various frameworks have been developed, there are similarities between the approaches. There is growing emphasis on stakeholder participation in the process. As there still is a lack of consensus and regulatory acceptance, implementation in the field has been impacted. More recently, resilience quantification metrics are being added to established frameworks to address both sustainability and resilience. The role of geoenvironmental engineers in sustainable development has been undervalued but is critical for future generations for maintaining the quality of the geoenvironment for the sustainability of critical resources.
authors: Catherine N. Mulligan
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Displacement-Based seismic Analysis of Underground Lifelines Using Response Surface-Based Strain Predictions
abstract: Underground lifelines are critical infrastructure essential for economic development and daily life. Earthquake-induced damage can significantly disrupt communities and cause substantial secondary losses, with repairs often requiring considerable time and expense. Despite the importance of seismic performance assessments, Korea currently lacks well-established design standards comparable to those of the American Lifelines Alliance. As strong domestic earthquakes become more frequent, the need for reliable seismic evaluation and design of underground lifelines has become urgent. Since underground lifelines deform together with the surrounding soil during seismic events, displacement-based static analysis is more appropriate than dynamic analysis. This study estimates ground displacements using ground strains derived from wave propagation theory, accounting for the spatial distribution of seismic effects. These displacements are then translated into loads using the Winkler spring model and applied in finite element analysis. A total of 4,000 analyses were conducted to develop response surfaces using second-order polynomial regression within the response surface method framework. The resulting models enable efficient strain estimation without requiring additional detailed analyses. This approach supports the development of practical seismic assessment guidelines for high-risk areas and offers a cost-effective tool for preliminary design and safety evaluation.
authors: Seungchan Oh, Kyungrok Kwon, Yangrok Choi, Youngjae Bae, Jung Sik Kong
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: A BIM-based 3D Design Compliance Review System for Power Grid Projects
abstract: Based on the background of smart grid and information construction, this study addresses the inefficiency and inaccuracy of traditional manual compliance review in BIM-based power grid engineering, particularly stemming from the laborious and error-prone human interpretation of complex, textual design specifications. To bridge this gap, Natural Language Processing (NLP) and deep learning are employed to automatically extract, interpret, and formalize complex textual specifications into quantifiable design rules. This process builds a specification knowledge base, which is then integrated with a BIMBase-based review platform, enabling real-time compliance checking. By mapping model components to specification clauses, the system supports automatic validation and error feedback. A case study on a Xuzhou substation demonstrates the platform’s effectiveness. Quantitative results show that the automated system reviewed 720 components in 30 minutes—six times faster than manual review—and detected 80 problematic components versus 52 manually, achieving an F1 score of 89.06%. The proposed method improves the accuracy, efficiency, and standardization of compliance checks. This research provides a practical solution for intelligent BIM review in power grid engineering, contributing to safer and more reliable infrastructure development.
authors: Lizhong Qi, Su Zhang, Jingguo Rong, Xing He, Xiangzhong Li
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Numerical simulation of extended end-plate connections under combined loading and bolt pretension
abstract: This study employs advanced finite element modeling to investigate the response of extended end-plate beam-to-column connections subjected to combined loading conditions, including bending moments, axial forces, and bolt pretension. The moment–rotation curve of the finite element model was validated against experimental data generated by De Lima, yielding an average error of 15.10% for the configuration with reinforcement plates at the load application point. The geometric model integrates standardized components based on DIN ( Deutsches Institut für Normung ) standards, such as bolts (DIN 933), nuts (DIN 934), and washers (DIN 125A), adhering to DIN 69 specifications for plate and column holes. An initial rotational stiffness of 10,622 kN∙m∙rad⁻¹ was achieved, with a validation error of 11.72%. This numerical approach provides a robust framework for assessing the behavior of semi-rigid joints under combined loading conditions. It also enables the identification of critical stress concentrations, supporting the use of stiffeners—as recommended by design codes—to enhance connection performance. These findings advance the understanding of semi-rigid connection mechanics and contribute to their optimization in structural engineering practice.
authors: Israel Díaz-Velazco, Alexandre Almeida Del Savio
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Research on Roof Damage and High-Strength Reinforcement in Deep Large-Section Mining Roadways
abstract: To address the challenge of controlling roof stability in large-section longwall mining roadways of deep mines, this paper establishes a roof mechanical model, derives a calculation method for the bearing capacity of bolted support systems, designs a novel high-strength grouted anchor bolt, and proposes a synergistic control strategy of “single-stage support with deep anchoring and shallow grouting.” The results indicate that roof stability is primarily governed by two key factors: the rock tensile strength (internal factor) and the support strength (external factor). The bearing capacity of the bolt support system increases with enhanced rock mechanical parameters but significantly weakens as bolt spacing increases. The newly developed high-strength grouted bolt, based on the aforementioned mechanism, combines high pre-tensioning anchoring with grouting reinforcement. Field tests demonstrated that it reduced surrounding rock deformation to 42.2% of the original support scheme, significantly enhancing overall roof stability.
authors: Wei Gong, Rui Pan, Mingyang Reng, Hongtao Wang, Junwei Li
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Experimental and Numerical Investigation on Mechanical Properties of Rock-Filled Concrete
abstract: Currently, there is no established standard for calculating the mechanical properties of rock-filled concrete (RFC), which are typically determined through large-scale tests. However, these tests are costly and challenging, making widespread implementation impractical. Therefore, based on the experimental results of an RFC engineering project, this paper established a 3D mesoscale model of RFC measuring 2000 mm × 2000 mm × 2000 mm to simulate the compressive strength and elastic modulus of RFC. The simulation clarified the mesoscale failure process of RFC and revealed the size-dependent variation in its strength. Through comparison with experimental results, the accuracy of the mesoscale simulation results was verified, demonstrating the potential of numerical simulation as a cost-effective and efficient method for estimating the mechanical properties of RFC.
authors: Shuang Zou, Youbin Li, Li Wu, Xuedan Lan, Yihui Liang
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Future-Proofing Curb Space: An AHP-Based Prioritization Framework for Flex Zone Use Cases in the Era of Autonomous Mobility
abstract: As autonomous vehicles (AVs) reshape the urban mobility landscape, cities face growing challenges in utilizing surplus road space created by improved traffic efficiency. This study examines the potential of Flex Zone, a dynamic and multifunctional curb space concept, in accommodating emerging mobility demands. While recent frameworks have begun to incorporate future-oriented modes such as AVs, Shared Autonomous Vehicles (SAVs), Urban Air Mobility, and micromobility, a systematic approach to organizing these functions remains limited. To address this, we present a restructured typology of 14 Flex Zone use cases that integrate diverse technologies and operational purposes. Using the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP), the study prioritizes this use cases based on their functional relevance. The results indicate that Public Transit Mobility (0.119), Pedestrian Mobility (0.106), and AV Mobility (0.103) are the top mobility priorities, while Designated Special Purpose Spaces (0.108), including emergency response and charging areas, emerged as essential non-mobility components. Preference variations were also observed across time of day, road hierarchy, and land-use contexts. Building on these findings, the study introduces an AHP-based spatial allocation framework that guides the strategic planning and management of curb space in AVs-oriented urban environments.
authors: Chaione KIM, J. W. Park, Junghwa Kim
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Mechanical analysis of steel tube-confined reinforced concrete double-column piers
abstract: To study the seismic performance of steel tube-confined reinforced concrete (STCRC) double-column piers, numerical models for two steel tube split configurations (Mode A and Mode B) were established in ABAQUS, to systematically analyze the effects of axial compression ratio and other parameters. Stiffness degradation is typically governed by the axial compression ratio, and split patterns show negligible effects. Numerical simulations accurately replicate experimental failure modes, thereby confirming the plastic hinge formation at predicted locations. Mode A is characterized by the plastic hinge forming at G1, while in Mode B, due to the introduction of a mid-height slit, the plastic hinge shifts to G2. Parametric studies reveal strong linear correlations between the peak lateral load capacity and the three key parameters (connecting plate height, reinforcement ratio, and axial compression ratio), while concrete strength and steel properties exhibit relatively weaker effects. The proposed simplified theoretical model can accurately predict the lateral load capacity, with errors not exceeding 8% compared to experimental results, demonstrating promising accuracy and application potential.
authors: Haicui Wang, Zhongxia Peng, Xuhong Zhou, Wei Guo, Pengkun Liu
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Bridging the Safety Gap: A Systematic Review of Traditional and Smart Personal Protective Equipment in Construction Safety
abstract: Construction remains one of the most hazardous sectors worldwide, with high rates of injuries and fatalities despite the longstanding use of traditional Personal Protective Equipment (PPE). This study systematically reviews the limitations of conventional PPE, evaluates emerging smart PPE technologies, and identifies barriers hindering adoption in construction. Guided by PRISMA 2020 protocols, 33 peer-reviewed journal articles published from 2015 to 2024 were retrieved from Scopus and Web of Science and analyzed through content and scientometric methods, including co-occurrence analysis using VOSviewer. Findings show traditional PPE is limited by human-centered issues (discomfort, improper fit, compliance gaps) and system-centered shortcomings (lack of real-time monitoring, durability problems, quality inconsistencies). In contrast, smart PPE integrating IoT, sensors, AI, and real-time analytics enables enhanced hazard detection, proactive alerts, and improved compliance. The most frequently reported limitation was discomfort and poor ergonomic fit, while IoT-enabled wearables particularly smart helmets and safety-vests were the most commonly evaluated smart PPE types. To address these challenges, a proposed conceptual framework integrates smart PPE adoption with safety culture and behavior, enabling predictive risk management. This study synthesizes current knowledge, identifies gaps, and provides a roadmap for future research involving Bayesian Belief Networks and Systems-Theoretic Process Analysis to advance safety standards.
authors: Muhammad Mubasher, Wei Tong Chen, Hew Cameron Merrett, Bangun Marpaung
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Seismic Performance Optimization Design of Concrete Beam-column Joints Based on NSGA-II Multi-objective Optimization Algorithm
abstract: In modern building construction, reinforced concrete beam-column frame structures serve as the primary load-bearing system in high-rise projects, where the performance of individual components critically influences overall structural integrity. Current design practices predominantly rely on empirical code-based data and single-objective safety factor constraints, an approach that often leads to underutilized material properties and design inefficiency. When confronting the high-dimensional, nonlinear optimization problems inherent in seismic performance design, conventional methods exhibit low computational efficiency and frequently yield poorly distributed solution sets, thereby failing to effectively balance competing objectives such as safety, cost, and environmental impact. To address these limitations - specifically low efficiency and uneven solution distribution in high-dimensional nonlinear problems - this study enhances the NSGA-II framework (Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II) with Pareto optimality principles. The proposed improvements incorporate an adaptive parameter-tuning strategy and a Kriging surrogate model to improve algorithmic convergence efficiency. Innovatively, this work integrates seismic performance metrics, Life Cycle Cost (LCC), and carbon emissions into a unified multi-objective optimization system to establish a more comprehensive design evaluation criterion. By employing a Deep Neural Network (DNN) to accelerate Finite Element Method (FEM) simulations, computational time for optimization iterations is reduced by over 65%. The results demonstrate that the optimized beam-column designs achieve material cost reductions of 12%-18% while maintaining equivalent seismic fortification levels and satisfying the seismic detailing requirements of ACI 318-19. The proposed multi-objective synergistic optimization approach overcomes the limitations of traditional single-objective design methods.
authors: Lin Xiang, Yi Bai, Kaixuan He
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Reducing the Uncertainty Assessment Factors in Discharge Measurement by Using the Velocity-Area Method Based on ISO Standards
abstract: The velocity-area method is commonly used to measure discharge in small rivers, typically using wading techniques with acoustic or propeller current meters. However, the accuracy of this method is influenced by several factors, including the number of verticals, the number of measurement points per vertical, measurement duration, and depth. ISO 748 (ISO, 2021) and ISO 1088 (ISO, 2007) offer guidelines for applying the velocity-area method and estimating discharge uncertainty based on these factors. While widely used, the uncertainty values provided in these standards were derived from field measurements conducted in the 1960s using propeller current meters in uncontrolled natural river conditions. These values have since been applied globally without further validation. This study aims to re-evaluate the errors associated with key measurement factors outlined in ISO 748 and 1088 through a controlled, real-scale experiment. The experiment was conducted at the KICT River Experiment Center in a stable, gentle-slope channel (A2) where flow conditions could be precisely managed. Discharge measurements were taken using micro-acoustic Doppler velocimeters (ADV) and total stations to assess the reliability of existing ISO-based uncertainty estimations under controlled conditions.
authors: Jongmin Kim, D. Kim
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Failure behaviour and crack propagation mechanism of non-persistent rough joints under direct shear: Insights from FEM–CZM method
abstract: • Analyzes how JRC, aperture and normal stress affect non-persistent rough joints. • Peak and residual shear stresses rise with JRC and fall as joint aperture increases. • FEM–CZM shows JRC, aperture and normal stress modify crack paths and failure modes. Joint roughness and aperture critically influence the shear failure and crack propagation of non-persistent joints, affecting rock mass strength and stability. This study develops a non-persistent joint model in ABAQUS using a finite element–cohesive zone model (FEM-CZM) method, where cracks are represented by deleting failed cohesive elements. The effects of joint roughness coefficient (JRC), aperture, and normal stress on shear response, stress evolution, and crack propagation are analyzed. Results show that peak and residual shear stresses increase with JRC and normal stress, whereas peak shear stress decreases with aperture; residual shear stress exhibits no clear trend with aperture. Cracks initiate at rock bridge tips; higher JRC promotes earlier initiation and wider propagation. Larger apertures localize cracks and increase tensile crack proportions. Higher normal stress accelerates crack coalescence, shifts failure from tensile to shear-dominated modes, and produces straighter shear surfaces. Total crack count rises with JRC and normal stress but decreases and the stabilizes with aperture. Tensile cracks generally account for about 65–80% of all cracks and dominate near rock bridges, while shear cracks concentrate near joint surfaces. These insights provide guidance for stability assessment and the design of reinforcement measures in rock masses with non-persistent joints.
authors: Chao Wang, X. Sun, Tingfang Liu, Shubo Zhang
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: The Analysis of Different Partial Wear Degrees of Cutter on the Rock-Breaking Effect
abstract: When the tunnel boring machine (TBM) is engaged in rock-breaking operations, the cutters are subject to wear due to the harsh underground environment. This study aims to investigate the effects of cutter wear on rock-breaking force and overall rock fragmentation efficiency. Firstly, a three-dimensional simulation model of the cutter breaking rock is generated through PFC3D. Then, the rock-breaking force, specific energy consumption and the internal crack breaking mode of the rock are studied under different partial wear degrees of the cutter. Finally, the simulation results were verified and analyzed through indoor scale-down experiments. The research results show that: 1) When the partial wear of the cutter is 8%, the normal force and tangential force increase by 29.1 kN and 12.2 kN, respectively, compared to those without wear. 2) The unit specific energy consumption increases continuously with cutter partial wear. At the 8% wear threshold, the inter-cutter synergistic effect is completely lost, and 91% rock ridge are formed. 3) When the partial wear occurs on the cutter, it will cause a change in the rock fragmentation form, from intergranular cracking to transgranular cracking.
authors: Peng Zhou, Jianxu Shang, Zinan Wang, Jiacan Xu, Haoxuan Zhao
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Prevent Insolvency in Construction Projects: A Strategic System Dynamics Model for Managing Line-of-Credit Financing
abstract: • Develops a system dynamics model for strategic LOC financing in construction • Simulates dynamic feedback between LOC policies and project cash flow behavior • Evaluates credit limit, withdrawal threshold, and repayment interval strategies • Integrates performance scoring to identify financially optimal LOC configurations • Enhances contractors’ ability to align cash flow strategy with project objectives Cash flow instability exposes contractors to significant short-term financing risks, often escalating into insolvency when borrowing decisions are made without understanding their dynamic effects on project liquidity. Before relying on bank financing, contractors need tools that reveal how credit limits, repayment terms, and financing costs influence cash-flow behavior under real project conditions. This study presents a System Dynamics framework for analyzing and optimizing Line of Credit (LOC) configurations in construction projects. It supports strategic-level decision-making by evaluating how LOC parameters affect key performance indicators, including profit, project duration, net present value of net cash flow, peak overdraft, and average net cash flow. The framework captures feedback-driven dynamics under volatile conditions and is implemented in Vensim PLE 8.2.1. Using causal loop and stock-flow diagrams, it simulates interactions between financing decisions and cash flow outcomes. A real hospital project (Reinforcement operation, $1.66 million project over 720 days) is used to validate the model and assess policy impacts. Results show that a moderate credit limit increased final profit by $10K, improved average net cash flow by $95K, and reduced peak overdraft by nearly $70K. The model provides an evidence-based decision framework for evaluating LOC strategies, managing financial risks, and improving cash flow resilience.
authors: Mehdi Arbati, Shahin Dabirian, Ali Katebi
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Impact of Artificial Precipitation on Stream Water Quality During Snowmelt Season
abstract: This study evaluates the impact of wintertime ground-based cloud seeding on stream water quality using data from the Odaecheon-1 site, located downstream of the Cloud Physics Observation Site (CPOS) in Daegwallyeong, South Korea. Since 2006, CPOS has conducted winter cloud-seeding experiments using silver iodide (AgI) and calcium chloride (CaCl 2 ). March water quality data from the baseline period (1997–2005, before the onset of cloud-seeding operations) and the seeding period (2006–2024, after the onset) were analyzed. Time-series analysis revealed increases in water temperature, electrical conductivity (EC), and nitrate nitrogen (NO₃⁻-N), along with decreasing trends in dissolved oxygen (DO) and biochemical oxygen demand (BOD). Seeding-period data also showed greater variability in EC and slightly declining ammonium nitrogen (NH₄⁺-N), suggesting shifts in nitrogen transformation pathways. Correlation analysis revealed a reorganization of inter-variable relationships after seeding began: classical eutrophication linkages such as water temperature–BOD and BOD–Total Phosphorus weakened or reversed, while associations between EC and nitrogen species (EC–Total Nitrogen, EC–NO₃⁻-N) strengthened. The findings suggest that winter cloud seeding may affect not only individual water quality parameters but also the structural relationships among them, potentially altering ecosystem response mechanisms. These results highlight the need for long-term, structure-based monitoring to assess indirect impacts of weather modification technologies.
authors: Eunsil Jung, Kilsoo Hyun
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Influence of Structural Details on Wind Uplift Capacity of Standing Seam Galvalume Roofs
abstract: Standing seam metal roof systems are widely used in various types of large-scale public buildings, but the key parameters influencing their wind resistance mechanisms have not been fully clarified. Elucidating these effects is crucial for enhancing the wind reliability and durability of the roofing system. This paper presents rigid wind tunnel tests and dynamic as well as static metal roof wind uplift tests on an airport traffic center, and investigates the influence of detailed structural parameters on the wind uplift capacity using numerical simulations. The results show that the wind load on the large-span roof cantilevered section of the traffic center is very large, and the most unfavorable negative wind pressure extremes for the areas without and with anti-wind clips are -2.72 kPa and -4.64 kPa, respectively. During the dynamic wind uplift test, the vertical displacement of the roof panel increases more rapidly after a 50 % loading ratio. During the static wind uplift test, the vertical displacement of the roof panel changes abruptly when the roof panel fails. The ultimate loads of the roof panel in areas without and with anti-wind clips are 5.6 kPa and 10.5 kPa, respectively. The wind load uplift capacity of the roof system is correlated with the detailed structural parameters, and the width of the semi-circular end of the fixed support has the most significant effect on the wind load uplift capacity of the roof panel. Therefore, it is necessary to consider the effect of these detailed structural parameters on the wind resistance of the roof panel system in the design.
authors: Rui Zhao, G. Y. Li, Dong Wu, Danhui DAN, Yifan He
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Full Life Cycle Risk Evaluation of the Weiyan High-Speed Railway Based on the DEMATEL-FT-BN Method
abstract: To address the critical research gaps in current high-speed railway risk assessment, investigate the safety status throughout the life cycle of the Weiyan high-speed railway and ensure construction, operational, and maintenance security, a three-phase analytical framework is developed. Firstly, the Decision-Making Trial and Evaluation Laboratory (DEMATEL) and Interpretative Structural Modeling (ISM) are combined to sort risk factors into clear hierarchical relationships, identifying key causal factors and their influence paths. Secondly, Fuzzy Set Theory (FT) is used to quantify uncertain expert judgments into reliable prior and conditional risk probabilities, solving the problem of ambiguous risk likelihood descriptions. Finally, Bayesian Network (BN) analysis is applied to model dynamic risk spread, enabling pre-accident risk prediction, in-process critical factor identification, and post-accident cause diagnosis. The main conclusions are as follows: (1) The full-life cycle risk of the Weiyan project is moderate, with a 59.7% probability during construction and 65.2% during operation-maintenance. (2) Sensitivity analysis identifies two top-priority risks: ineffective leakage of goaf grouting and high-speed railway equipment signal failure. (3) Post-accident diagnostic analysis shows that the main causal factors include insufficient goaf grouting depth, grouting leakage, ground subsidence anomalies, equipment signal failure, and improper railway line operation. Additionally, this framework helps practitioners prioritize controls and policymakers formulate full-life cycle safety standards, filling cross-phase risk linkage gaps in existing models.
authors: Daman Niu, Yuan Wang, Xuemin Li, Zhiqing Zhang, Wen Li
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Evolutionary Symbolic Models for Predicting Mortar Strength Incorporating Plastic Waste and Supplementary Cementitious Materials for Sustainable Construction
abstract: This study used cutting-edge machine learning methods to create models that could predict the compressive strength (C-S) of plastic-based mortar (PBMs). The methods used included Gene Expression Programming (GEP) and Multi-Expression Programming (MEP). Compared to more conventional black-box machine learning techniques, evolutionary symbolic regression models have the benefit of producing clear mathematical equations that reveal the connections between input parameters and output strength. The models were trained and validated using a large dataset that included various PBMs mix proportions. Hyperparameter tuning was performed to optimize model performance and improve predictive accuracy. The MEP model achieved a higher R² value of 0.940 than the GEP model, which was at 0.913. The predictive capabilities of both models were further validated using statistical performance metrics and cross-validation using Taylor's diagram. A rigorous sensitivity analysis was conducted to quantify the influence of key mix components on C-S. The findings of this study provide valuable insights into optimizing PBMs formulations for enhanced C-S, reducing dependency on conventional cement, and promoting sustainable construction practices. The results also establish the effectiveness of GEP and MEP as reliable predictive tools for evaluating and improving PBMs performance in real-world applications.
authors: Muhammad Iftikhar Faraz, Sk. Mohammad Zaheer, S. A. Khan, Muhammad Tahir Qadir, Syed Jibran Shah Bokhari
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Hazard Mitigation Approach for Extreme Flooding based on Inundation Mapping
abstract: The Upper Indus Basin (UIB), located in Hindu-Kush Karakoram Himalaya (HKH) region, is predicted to experience increase in precipitation and consequently high inflows according to Representative Concentration Pathways (RCP) 4.5 and 8.5 at global and regional warming thresholds of 1.5 °C and 2.0 °C. Tarbela dam, an outlet for UIB, on the river Indus in Pakistan is one of the biggest earth fill dams in the world having a reservoir capacity of 16.3 billion cubic meters. This study assesses the hazard associated with the Tarbela dam failure through inundation mapping and proposes a comprehensive hazard mitigation plan. The mitigation plan includes details on hazard and risk assessment, early warning and evacuation, and disaster relief efforts. To achieve this, HEC-RAS hydraulic modeling was applied under a hypothetical dam failure scenario triggered by overtopping, providing a high-resolution estimation of inundation extents and depths. In addition, the study uniquely integrates hazard mapping with a risk assessment matrix for emergency response planning, offering a practical framework. The major focus of the investigation is inundation mapping through hydraulic modeling that is performed by HEC-RAS under the hypothetical dam failure caused by overtopping. The findings and recommendations of the study can help the decision makers in developing and implementing a robust emergency action plan for dam-break scenarios and extreme flooding.
authors: Waqas Arshad Tanoli, Khawar Rehman, Muhammad Umer Zubair, Seung Ho Hong
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Vibration and noise measurement of metro depot under urban comprehensive traffic
abstract: To promote the process of urban construction, China is vigorously developing the over-track buildings above the metro depot. However, the traffic-induced vibration and noise of over-track buildings greatly affect the quality of life of resident, which should be controlled to ensure the living comfort of over-track buildings. Relying on a practical over-track building project over a large-scale metro depot in Southwest China, the accelerations of rail, sleeper, column, load-bearing column and cover plate in the throat area and inspection area of the depot are measured, as well as the traffic-induced noise around the depot. The propagation characteristics of train-induced vibration and traffic-induced noise are investigated by analyzing the test data using spectral, 1/3 octave spectral, and maximum Z-vibration levels, and their influences on the planned over-track buildings is evaluated. Experimental results show that the main frequency of rail vibration is around 840Hz, and that of sleeper, column, load-bearing column and cover plate vibrations is in the range of 0-200Hz. The vibration in the throat area is obviously greater than the that in the inspection area. Along the longitudinal direction of the line, the vibration at the two ends of the metro depot is larger, and the that in the middle of the metro depot is smaller. In the process of vibration transmission from the rail to the cover plate, the vibration decreases first and then increases. Vibration transmission from rail to sleeper(column) is reduced by 18-31dB, and vibration transmission from sleeper(column) to load-bearing column is reduced by 10.6-33dB, while that from load-bearing column to cover plate is increased by 2-4dB. The maximum Z-vibration levels of the load-bearing column in throat area, the load-bearing column in inspection area and the cover plate are 63.2dB, 61.2dB and 67.3dB respectively, and the vibration of cover plate exceeds the limit by 0.3dB. The noise of the metro depot during the nighttime is more serious than that during the daytime. The maximum sound pressure levels of the inspection area, throat area, Haixia road and metro elevated during the nighttime are 62.5dB, 64.9dB(A), 78.1dB(A) and 77.0dB(A), exceeding the limit by 17.5dB(A), 19.9dB(A), 23.1dB(A) and 22dB(A) respectively. To ensure the quality of life of residents, the project urgently needs to take vibration and noise reduction measures and select according to the needs..
authors: Jing Tang, Zhaowei Chen, Song Peng, Qianhua Pu, Hong Xu
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Multidimensional Signal-Aided Machine Learning for Void Detection in Concrete-Filled Steel Tubes
abstract: Concrete-Filled Steel Tube (CFST) arch bridges are highly vulnerable to debonding defects, which pose serious threats to structural safety. Existing detection methods are often limited by low accuracy, noise susceptibility, and a strong reliance on empirical judgment. To address these issues, this study proposes an intelligent debonding detection framework based on adaptive signal analysis and Machine Learning. The Grasshopper Optimization Algorithm (GOA) was employed to automatically optimize the parameters of Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD), establishing a GOA-VMD adaptive signal decomposition framework that effectively overcomes the challenges of mode mixing and parameter dependence inherent in traditional methods. Weak debonding features were further enhanced using the Teager Energy Operator, while high-resolution time–frequency analysis was achieved through the synchrosqueezed wavelet transform. A composite evaluation index combining Cyclic Kurtosis Entropy (CKE) was developed to construct a robust feature vector, which serves as the input for a Gradient Boosting Tree (GBT) classifier. Comparative analysis demonstrates that this feature-driven machine learning approach significantly outperforms conventional machine learning (Artificial Neural Network) models in terms of accuracy and robustness. Numerical simulations and feature analyses yielded the following findings: (1) a co-located measurement scheme (impact and sensor at the same point) improved detection stability by 72%; (2) measurement location was identified as the most influential factor for detection accuracy, contributing 38%; and (3) sensitivity for detecting debonding thicker than 4 mm reached 98%. The study establishes a reliable debonding identification technique that can be directly integrated into bridge health monitoring systems, offering an efficient tool for engineering applications.
authors: Bingjian Wang, Benyu Wang, Ke Chen, Longbang Qing, Ke Chen
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Systematic Waterlogging Prevention and Control Design for a Mountainous City in Southwest China
abstract: This study presents a systematic strategy for preventing waterlogging in a mountainous city in Southwest China. By employing watershed-based planning, the causes of frequent waterlogging are analyzed, and a comprehensive solution combining engineering and non-engineering measures is developed. This approach integrates green, gray and blue infrastructure and governance, hereafter referred to as the Green–Gray–Blue–Governance approach. Key measures include source runoff reduction, process flow control, and emergency response planning for extreme rainfall events. SWMM simulations show that the proposed upgrades significantly improve discharge capacity and reduce waterlogging risks, providing valuable insights for similar urban drainage projects.
authors: Hao Cheng, Jing Bai
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Optimizing Concrete Design in High-Rise Buildings Through Adaptive Parametric BIM: A Mixed Methods Framework
abstract: This paper presents an adaptive parametric Building Information Modeling (BIM) method for enhancing concrete design of tall buildings. Traditional BIM workflows are great at representing and coordinating but struggle to incorporate near real-time feedback and iterative performance-based design exercises. Our methodology fills this gap by implementing parametric control (Autodesk Revit), structural analysis (ETABS), interference detection (Navisworks), and statistical endorsement (SPSS) in a closed-loop process. The framework validation was conducted in two ways: a qualitative benchmark of Shanghai and Canton Towers and a quantitative simulation of a 30-story prototype model. Performance metrics volumetric coverage, seismic response, cost savings, and workflow efficiency were monitored between iterations. Results revealed that the required concrete volume was reduced by 14%, seismic performance was improved by 13%, and all design conflicts were resolved. What’s transformative of this research, is to show that adaptive BIM can go beyond a static design tool or a mechanism of design coordination, to become a facility to achieve dynamic optimization. By facilitating early design stage performance-based decision making, the framework provides a scalable and data-driven approach to sustainable and efficient high-rise construction. The originality of the study lies in combining simulation analytics with parametric modeling tools to return empirically validated and repeatable outcomes for the AEC sector.
authors: Oriah Mudondo, Chunyan Yuan, Zhang Chengyu, Wang Yan, Sun Xueyuan
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Prediction Model for Development Length of Corroded Prestressing Strand Considering Rotation-affected Bond Failure Mechanism
abstract: Evaluation of corrosion’s effect on the development length of prestressing strand is crucial for pretensioned prestressed concrete structures serving in the aggressive environment. This paper presents an analytical model for predicting the development length of corroded prestressing strand, in which the effects of strand corrosion on the transfer length and flexural bond length are considered separately. The effect of corrosion on the initial prestressing transfer length is analyzed by considering the combined effects of initial releasing cracking and subsequent corrosion-induced cracking on the evolution of confinement around the prestressing strand. Thereafter, beyond the transfer stage, the effect of strand corrosion on the additional flexural bond length is confirmed based on the calculation of maximum bond strength for various corrosion degrees, in which the effect of strand rotation along its axis is considered for the pull-out failure mode in a well-confined condition. Comparison of analytical and experimental results shows that this model can reasonably predict the development length of corroded prestressing strand.
authors: Ju Yi, Lei Wang, Ming Deng, Guodong Wang
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Visual Perception Analysis of Yanji Streetscape Using Eye-Tracking Technology
abstract: This study focuses on the streetscapes of Yanji as its subject of investigation. It methodically examines the visual perception attributes and preferences of urban spatial components by integrating eye-tracking technology with subjective assessment. Five core elements, namely buildings, streets, greenery, landscape nodes, and landmark, were selected to construct visual stimulus materials containing 15 sets of typical scenes. The attention allocation and visual exploration patterns of 62 subjects were recorded using EyeSo Ec30 eye-tracker. The exploration pattern was supplemented with a two-dimensional questionnaire (design aesthetics and perception dimensions, functional and environmental dimensions) to obtain a subjective evaluation. (2) Internet-famous destination and gazebo achieve visual compensation effect in the low green environment through spatial innovation, and their proportion of fixation time and aesthetic appeal score are significantly higher than that of standardized commercial landmarks; (3) Street advertisements, signage, and ethnic architecture interface in the landscape form visual competition, and the core attraction of plants elements in street trees verifies the key regulatory role of ecological design on urban imagery.
authors: Wanqiao Lv, Jiahe Zhang, Ruixing Lin, Yan Gao, Shunmei Piao
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Long-Term Evaluation of Acoustic Decay, Drainage, and Surface Resistance in Engineered Asphalt Pavements through Integrated Field and Laboratory Analysis
abstract: • Six-year field and laboratory study links acoustic, mechanical, and surface durability. • Porous Q-Pave achieved strongest initial noise reduction but lost function through clogging. • Duplex pavement balanced acoustic stability and rutting resistance via dual-layer design. • Medi-Temp dense mix offered the best long-term durability and skid resistance retention. • Findings guide pavement selection for urban noise mitigation and sustainable maintenance. Urban traffic noise remains a critical environmental issue, prompting the deployment of low-noise asphalt pavement solutions. However, most existing studies have examined only short-term acoustic or material properties of low-noise pavements, lacking integrated long-term evidence that connects acoustic decay, drainage performance, and surface resistance under real traffic and environmental conditions. To bridge this gap, this study conducts a comprehensive six-year field evaluation and laboratory correlation of three representative low-noise asphalt systems. This study presents a comprehensive six-year field evaluation of three asphalt surface types: Q-Pave (high-void porous asphalt), Duplex Pavement (double-layer system), and Medi-Temp Pavement (medium-temperature dense mix). Performance was assessed through close-proximity noise (CPX/Non-CPX), permeability (Bowler Test), skid resistance (British Pendulum Tester (BPT), Grip Tester), and durability (Tensile Strength Ratio (TSR), Hamburg Wheel Tracking (HWT), Cantabro). Q-Pave achieved the highest initial noise reduction (∼80.4 dB(A)) but showed moderate acoustic degradation (+1.2 dB/year) and declining surface functions. Duplex Pavement demonstrated strong acoustic stability (+1.35 dB/year) with consistent drainage and friction. Medi-Temp Pavement, despite a higher long-term CPX (∼72.9 dB(A)), retained excellent durability, minimal rutting, and 94% skid resistance retention. Notably, porosity showed strong correlation with both noise (R² = 0.97) and permeability (R² = 0.99). The findings emphasize the trade-offs between acoustic performance and long-term resilience, highlighting that while porous pavements provide early noise benefits, denser or layered systems better sustain safety and durability. These results offer valuable guidance for urban road planners seeking to balance environmental noise reduction with infrastructure longevity.
authors: Sang-Yum Lee, Tri Ho Minh Le
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Seismic resilience evaluation method of bridge considering restoration process
abstract: Based on the general method of life cycle analysis of concrete structures exposed to harsh environment, this method takes the time-varying seismic capability of each failure state as the functional index, and connects the residual function and recovery process after the disaster with the time of the earthquake event. By sampling the time-varying bridge fragility model to obtain the bridge earthquake failure samples, combined with the time-varying function index, the genetic algorithm is used to solve the resource-constrained project scheduling problem, the specific recovery process of the bridge after the earthquake is given, and finally the seismic resilience of the bridge during service is obtained. The results show that when the time-varying function is not considered, the calculated seismic resilience of the bridge is significantly greater than that of the bridge considering the time-varying function, which will overestimate the ability of the bridge to resist and recover from the earthquake, which is not conducive to the development of the post-earthquake restoration work. The selected control time ( t h - t 0 ) should be reasonable. If the control time ( t h - t 0 ) is too small, the calculated seismic resilience of the bridge is zero universally, and the seismic resilience of the bridge cannot be well express.
authors: Li Tinghui, Jing Ji, Zhang Shixian, Jinlong Liu, Lu Sun
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Analytical modeling for temperature effects on cable-stayed bridges based on elastic foundation beam theory
abstract: To investigate the effect of temperature on the structural behavior of cable-stayed bridges during the operational phase, an elastic foundation beam model is introduced into the temperature effect analysis. In this model, the girder is idealized as a beam on a continuous elastic foundation, with support stiffness derived from the axial stiffness of the stay cables. Temperature effects are equivalently modeled as loads acting on the beam, and based on the theory of elastic foundation beams, an expression for the deformation of the girder is derived, thereby establishing a simplified methodology for analyzing temperature effects in cable-stayed bridges. The proposed methodology is validated using measured temperature effect data from the Zengjiang Bridge. The results indicate that an increase in cable temperature induces a downward deflection of the girder, while an increase in the girder temperature results in upward deflection. Notably, the influence of cables temperature on girder deflection is substantially greater than that of the girder temperature itself, leading to a downward trend in overall deflection during daytime temperature rises, with the lowest point of the girder's shape occurring around 15:00 and the highest point around 5:00.
authors: Chunyu Fu, Bin Li, Yunpeng Liu
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Modeling for Predicting Thrust and Torque Requirement of Full-Face Blind Shaft Boring Machine with Conical Cutterhead
abstract: The power system selection and operating condition adaptability of the Full-face Blind Shaft Boring Machine (SBM) depend critically on the thrust and torque of its conical cutterhead. However, the complex forces acting on the conical cutterhead were not fully understood. This study analysed the shaft engineering conditions, including high ground stress, accumulated rock muck at the shaft bottom, and water presence at the shaft bottom. Based on this, the theoretical expressions for the thrust and torque were derived, considering the operational characteristics of rock breaking along the vertical conical surface and vertical excavation of conical cutterhead. Furthermore, theoretical load modeling for predicting thrust and torque requirement was established. The load modeling shows that the theoretical thrust accuracy of the conical cutterhead is 95.0 %, and the theoretical torque accuracy is 92.7 %, confirming its practical applicability. For the influence of shaft engineering conditions and cutterhead structural characteristics, the thrust required for rock-breaking increases with the cone angle. The resultant force of rock breaking influenced by the cone angle and the rotational resistance torque of the main bearing and the main seal need to be considered. Particularly, the passive earth pressure force and frictional resistance moment of the bottom panel need to be considered under the working condition of the large accumulation of muck at the bottom of the excavation face. The research results provide a reliable basis for the parameter selection of the power system during the SBM’s design phase.
authors: Hongbo Huang, Hong Guan, Yusheng Shen
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Theoretical Study on the Rock-support Interaction for Deep Tunnel Based on Unified Strength Criterion
abstract: The interaction between surrounding rock and support structure is fundamental for support structure design and tunnel stability evaluation, closely related to underground engineering safety and economy. A strain-softening model based on Unified Strength Criteria (USC) is employed to characterize deep rock masses, considering intermediate principal stress effects on rock mechanical behavior. Under the assumption of axisymmetric plane strain, a new mechanical model for rock-support interaction is established. A corresponding solution method is proposed and validated by numerical simulation results, existing analytical models and field datasets. Parameter studies were conducted to investigate the effects of intermediate principal stress and supporting time on rock-support interaction. The findings indicate that the intermediate principal stress has a significant impact on the stability of the surrounding rock and the load-sharing behavior of the support structure. The increasing intermediate principal stress significantly reduces both tunnel wall displacement and support load, with a Stress Effect Factor (SEF) demonstrating up to a 28.1% reduction in tunnel wall deformation and a 12.6% reduction in support load when transitioning from the traditional Mohr-Coulomb criterion (the intermediate principal stress effect coefficient b =0) to a double shear strength criterion ( b =1). The proposed model and solution method provide a comprehensive and accurate approach to analyzing rock-support interaction in deep tunnels, offering valuable insights for tunnel design and support structure optimization.
authors: Rui Pan, Mingyang Ren, Hua Cheng
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Actuator Placement in Cable-Strut Structures via Localized SVD and Multi-Objective Optimization
abstract: Cable-strut structures are highly sensitive to variations in member lengths, making them particularly suitable for active control. This study proposes a computational framework integrating local singular value decomposition (SVD) and multi-objective optimization to address the actuator placement design problem in cable-strut structures. Items corresponding to inactive members are set to zero to simplify the structural response sensitivity matrix, generating a reduced-dimensional local sensitivity matrix excluding invalid terms. Subsequently, local SVD is applied to efficiently calculate the optimal actuation force without iterative solving—while maintaining accuracy, the reduced-dimensional sensitivity formula significantly reduces computational cost. A multi-objective optimization module based on NSGA-III is adopted to determine the position, quantity, and actuation force of actuators, with optimization objectives including minimum displacement residual, minimum number of actuators, and uniform actuation force. The effectiveness of the framework is verified through case studies on three types of tensegrity structures. Results demonstrate that the proposed method can control over 80% of the target displacement while reducing the number of actuators by more than 60%.
authors: Jiang Cao, Mingliang Zhu, Jin Wang, Zhenhua Wang
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Prediction of the vortex-induced vibrations for hang-off drilling risers via integral transform
abstract: When the marine environmental conditions exceed the safe operating limits of the marine riser, the riser must be separated from the blowout preventer (BOP) and enter a suspended state until the conditions improve. This paper establishes a nonlinear dynamic model to describe the dynamic behavior of the suspended riser under harsh conditions. The model system takes into account the influence of the axial variation of stiffness, mass, and hydrodynamic effects on the vortex-induced vibration (VIV) characteristics of the suspended riser system with attached LMRP mass in the shear flow field. The governing equations are solved using the generalized integral transformation technique (GITT), which combines semi-analytical form with error-controlled numerical integration to ensure efficient and stable computation. The accuracy of the model and method is verified by comparing with experimental data from the literature. Further parameter analysis shows that the LMRP mass and the suspension length have significant effects on the time history response, natural frequency, and mode shape of the riser. The research results can provide a theoretical basis for the design and safety assessment of suspended risers in complex marine environments.
authors: Tongtong Li, Yi Zhang, Haocheng Ni, Wei Chun Wang, Weiping Feng
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Effect of temperature and sulfate erosion on mechanical properties and shrinkage characteristics of cement-stabilized macadam
abstract: Cement-stabilized macadam (CSM) is susceptible to deterioration and shrinkage cracking under fluctuating temperatures and salt erosion conditions. This study investigates the effects of ambient temperature, cement dosage, and sulfate content on the mechanical properties and shrinkage behavior of CSM. Compressive strength, splitting strength, dry and temperature shrinkage, hydration products, and microstructure of CSM were analyzed under varying conditions using mechanical tests, shrinkage tests, XRD, and SEM. Results indicated that increasing sulfate content led to more erosion products and the formation of internal cracks, which reduced mechanical performance. Higher curing temperature and cement dosage accelerated hydration, improved aggregate bonding and compaction, and enhanced mechanical properties. For sulfate-free CSM, dry shrinkage strain (DSS) mainly occurs during early dehydration, while temperature shrinkage strain (TSS) increases almost linearly as temperature decreases. In sulfate-containing CSM, DSS exhibits an initial increase followed by a decrease, whereas TSS first decreases and then increases with decreasing temperature. Based on these findings, DSS and TSS models incorporating cement dosage and sulfate content were developed to quantitatively characterize CSM shrinkage under various conditions. The shrinkage process of CSM under cooling and salt erosion can be divided into three stages, primarily reflecting the interaction between shrinkage stress and salt crystallization stress.
authors: Fuerhaiti Ainiwaer, Lei Tian, Zhao-Xuan Hao, Huizhou Li, Jie Li
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Research on the strata movement laws of deep multi-seam mining in super-thick and weak cementation overburden
abstract: Taking the Yingpanhao Coal Mine as the geological prototype, a FLAC-PFC coupled numerical model was established based on measured mechanical parameters. The model was calibrated and validated using field leveling data, and both macroscopic movement patterns and microscopic failure processes were analyzed in combination. This approach was applied to investigate in depth the influence of interlayer spacing and pillar offset on the strata movement mechanism during deep multi-seam mining under extremely thick and weakly cemented overburden. Results demonstrate a strong correlation between pillar offset and surface movement parameters. Increasing the offset reduces the maximum subsidence angle. As extraction progresses, the stress arch evolves from a single to a multi-arch structure, with surface subsidence following a Boltzmann function. An offset ≥45 m promotes a stable masonry beam structure that effectively controls subsidence, whereas a 30 m offset causes stress concentration and roof cutting. The optimal offset is determined to be 45 m, balancing pressure relief and settlement control. During repeated mining, a decreasing depth-to-thickness ratio enhances the load-bearing capacity of middle strata; however, at a ratio of 11, overall bending induces a sharp settlement increase. These findings provide theoretical and practical guidance for repeated mining under similar geological conditions..
authors: Li Fu, Guojian Zhang, Li Xing, Guangli Guo, Jiancai Sui
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Research and application of a novel strut-braced blast-resistant shelter in the retrofit of existing buildings
abstract: The blast-resistant shelter, as a highly safe and relatively independent blast-resistant reinforcement measure, has not been widely adopted due to its high cost and significant retrofitting difficulties. Based on the equivalent stress distribution at the steel frame beam-support member joints in the supporting blast-resistant shelter, an optimized design was conducted for the weak areas of the joints. A "rigid support + buffer device" buffered support member is proposed, where a buffer layer is embedded at the steel frame beam-support member interface to establish a buffering mechanism, while stiffener plates are symmetrically arranged in the stress concentration zone of the support beam web to form localized flexural stiffness enhancement zones. Parametric design was performed for different types of buffer materials, and the dynamic responses of both the support joints and the original structure during blast simulation were extracted. The results show that the buffer supporting blast-resistant shelter can better ensure that existing buildings are less affected by explosion loads, reducing the dynamic response amplitude of existing structural frame columns by about 25%. The application of the new type of the supporting blast-resistant shelter in the anti explosion reinforcement and renovation project example summarizes the anti explosion reinforcement process. The application of new supporting components not only ensures the anti explosion effect of the anti explosion shield, but also reduces the reinforcement cost by about 30%. This further verifies the engineering feasibility of the new supporting blast-resistant shelter and confirms that the support members incorporated in the innovative supporting blast-resistant shelter reinforcement system demonstrate substantial practical engineering value, it has high potential application value in military, chemical and other fields.
authors: Hongxu LIU, Hongkun Shang, Yuan Cao, Youhao Li, Weiqi Guo
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Impact of ESG disclosure on green innovation in construction enterprises: empirical evidence from chinese listed companies
abstract: Environmental, social, and corporate governance (ESG) is an important criterion for measuring corporate sustainability. ESG disclosure is beneficial for promoting green innovation in construction companies. Using data from listed construction companies on the Shanghai and Shenzhen A-share markets between 2010 and 2023, this study employs linear regression analysis and a two-way fixed-effects model to examine the impact of ESG disclosure on green innovation. Results indicate that ESG disclosure significantly enhances green innovation in construction companies, with each unit increase in ESG disclosure raising green innovation by 1.666 units (p < 0.05). Further research reveals that ESG disclosure can significantly enhance green innovation in construction companies through new quality productivity forces (NQPFs). Internal control and environmental regulation have a positive moderating effect on the relationship between ESG disclosure and green innovation in construction companies, with environmental regulation exerting a slightly stronger moderating effect than internal control. Heterogeneity analysis reveals that the impact of ESG disclosure on green innovation in construction companies is more pronounced among state-owned enterprises, small enterprises, enterprises in central China, and the building decoration industry. This study's findings provide a theoretical basis and practical implications for government policymaking and promotion of green innovation in construction companies.
authors: Ru Fa, Shengyang Zhang, Yanhua Li, Aiqing Zhang
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Text–model generation–machine learning framework and performance of three-sided protection steel beam temperature model
abstract: The three-sided protection steel beam is commonly used in offshore and onshore oil and gas processing facilities. The top surface of the upper flange is exposed, causing a rapid temperature decrease in the vertical direction, influenced by section depth, insulation thickness, and conductivities. Deriving an equation to encompass these factors is analytically challenging. A machine learning model with fast prediction is a viable approach, despite the labour-intensive modelling and database training processes. To address this, a machine learning-based prediction programme connects parameter inputs, modelling, simulation, and data extraction using Python, ABAQUS, and MATLAB. A model generation agent embedded with a 2D contact detection algorithm is produced to establish the beam heat transfer models. For performing the large-scale simulation, an interruptible MATLAB terminal is adopted to control the sequence. Results are processed through a data pipeline and trained with Gradient Boosting and Deep Neural Network (DNN). In addition, a data-batch generator is designed to reduce memory usage and enable training on a single GPU. The database comprises 414 models of IPE sections, Universal Beam sections, and 63 welded sections, with depths ranging from 127 to 1500mm. 400°C and 538°C steel limit temperatures are considered for the insulation configuration. Results show a root mean squared error of 1.34°C between the test and prediction results, with gradient boosting prevailing for regression problems under similar training times. The test calculation time reflects high computational efficiency, with 83.3% of predictions completed within 60 seconds.
authors: Yang Li, Yang You, Lele Sun, Peijun Wang
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: A review of ML for bridge vertical displacement
abstract: The vertical displacement of bridges is a crucial metric for assessing structural safety and operational performance. Accurate prediction of such displacement is indispensable for effective bridge management and timely risk warning. Nevertheless, traditional finite element methods (FEM) encounter significant limitations in practical applications, primarily due to intricate modeling processes and limited generalization capacity. With the accumulation of monitoring data and recent progress in artificial intelligence, machine learning (ML) approaches have demonstrated substantial potential in displacement prediction. This review synthesizes commonly adopted ML techniques for both non-temporal and temporal modeling, and provides a comparative analysis of their performance and applicability. It further classifies existing studies according to the types of input data—such as strain, temperature, traffic load, historical displacement, and multi-factor fusion—thereby elucidating how data characteristics shape model development. In addition, the review highlights emerging research directions, including explainable AI for bridge prediction models, spatial modeling of monitoring topology, and physics-data-driven modeling approaches. Collectively, these explorations establish a theoretical foundation and research agenda for developing more accurate and reliable predictive models.
authors: Yixin Chen, Qun Xie, Yanjun Li, Ruijun Liang, Li Zhu
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Influence of nano SiO2/Al2O3-TiO2 powder on strength and durability properties of concrete
abstract: This study investigates the application of titanium dioxide (TiO₂), a photocatalyst, to concrete by utilizing TiO₂-coated nano-SiO₂ (NSTP) and nano-Al₂O₃ (NATP) substrates. The incorporation of NSTP and NATP into concrete enhanced mechanical properties through pozzolanic reactions, generating calcium silicate hydrate (C-S-H) and calcium aluminate hydrate (C-A-H) gels that densified the matrix and reduced voids. Concrete containing NSTP and NATP exhibited higher compressive strength compared to conventional TiO₂-based concrete, as well as improved durability, including resistance to chemical attack, carbonation, and chloride ion penetration. Furthermore, under ultraviolet light (<380 nm), NSTP and NATP based concrete demonstrated 100–110% higher photocatalytic efficiency in nitrogen oxide decomposition compared to commercial TiO 2 based systems. These improvements are attributed to enhanced hydration product polymerization and optimized nanoparticle dispersion within the concrete matrix. The findings suggest that NSTP and NATP based concrete can serve as a multifunctional material for sustainable infrastructure by improving structural performance and environmental remediation such as nitrogen oxide decomposition reactions.
authors: Jong-Won Lee, Jong-Won Lee, Sung-Lin Yang
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Electric two-wheeler crashes: Risk factors and potential solutions – A systematic review
abstract: Electric two-wheelers have been developed as a sustainable alternative to reduce environmental pollution. Nevertheless, like conventional two-wheelers, they contribute significantly to road traffic crashes. While some risk factors associated with these crashes have been identified, others remain uncertain. Therefore, this review aims to summarize the available evidence regarding risk factors related to electric two-wheelers. We searched for studies on electric two-wheeler risk analysis published in English from January 2010 to September 2024 in multiple databases. In total, 61 studies met the inclusion criteria. Three domains of risk factors associated with electric two-wheelers were identified, including human (e.g., age, gender, behaviors), environment (e.g., time, weather, visibility, road conditions), and vehicle (e.g., size, weight, speed). The findings highlight that existing research has predominantly focused on rider demographics and behavioral factors, with significant attention given to age, gender, helmet use, and traffic violations. Moreover, in light of current research, the review highlights the need for future research to address data gaps by utilizing advanced monitoring techniques such as real-time systems, video analytics, and wearable devices. Furthermore, the review highlights the exploration of key factors such as psychological factors, risk perceptions, and urban infrastructure in the context of electric two-wheelers to enhance rider safety.
authors: Amjad Pervez, Duy Quy Nguyen-Phuoc, Jaeyoung Lee, Fangrong Chang, Humair Munir
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Experimental and numerical analysis of mechanical properties of steel beam-column joints with initial defects
abstract: The welding defects in the lower flange area of beam-column joints can easily cause brittle fracture, which can lead to structural failure. In this paper, an artificial defect was deliberately introduced into the single-beam-flange welds of beam-column assemblies. Five distinct loading protocols were then applied to the test specimens to investigate how the initial flaw and the loading regime jointly affect the mechanical response of the welded joints. Furthermore, the Extended Finite Element Method (XFEM) was employed to simulate the experimental process. A comparative analysis between experimental and XFEM results was conducted. The results indicate that the initial weld defect causes significant differences in the crack propagation rate and location, and decreases the bearing capacity and energy dissipation of the joints. The performance of the joint is independent of the loading span, but the sudden displacement loading can cause greater damage. The XFEM can accurately reflect the mechanical performance of the joints, particularly under cyclic loading conditions with initial defects.
authors: Yuan Zuo, Weibin Li
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Numerical study of seismic performance of irregular continuous reinforced concrete rigid-frame bridges
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive numerical investigation into the seismic performance of continuous reinforced concrete rigid-frame (CRCR) bridges with geometric irregularities. Based on a real case study in Iran, several bridge models were developed in OpenSees by systematically varying span length ratios, pier height ratios, and pier skew angles. Both modal and nonlinear analyses were conducted, and the modeling approach was validated using experimental data. Results indicate that increasing the pier height ratio reduces seismic capacity, with the most critical configuration (B16) showing approximately a 45% reduction in the longitudinal direction and 40% in the transverse direction compared to the reference bridge (B0). Conversely, reducing the difference between adjacent span lengths improves the lateral load-bearing capacity by up to 55% longitudinally and 45% transversely. Increasing skew angles leads to longer natural periods—by as much as 50%—due to torsional effects. These findings highlight the significant impact of geometric irregularities on dynamic behavior and point out the need for advanced seismic design strategies and targeted retrofitting measures to improve the resilience of irregular CRCR bridges in earthquake-prone regions.
authors: Seyed Hadi Rashedi, Alireza Rahai
journal: KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering

title: Application of ChatGPT in seepage-induced slope stability
abstract: ChatGPT has been recently applied to many engineering fields including geotechnical engineering. This study investigates the chance of using ChatGPT for seepage-induced slope stability problems based on the grid and radius method. The Python code for solving seepage and slope analysis was first generated, followed by the coupling of those two analyses was performed using ChatGPT.
